body_txt: Simply put, this is what happens when you have a famous or otherwise successful person with a sibling who feels overshadowed by their success. The feelings may or may not be justified. It is possible that all siblings involved are equally popular, but one of them simply feels as if they are getting shafted. Even if they get all sorts of media attention, they could constantly complain about their perceived "more famous" relative. And on the flip side, there may be times where this is justified because the character doesn't have the same amount of success or the army of adoring fans. They will either remain loyal to their sibling despite being insecure (it will help if they have a close relationship) or they may be overcome with intense jealousy and will plot to get themselves the same amount of attention, with varying degrees of success. It could also be a simple case of a character who is expected by everyone to follow the same path as their (often, but not always older) sibling, whether they want to or not, and are under intense pressure to honor the "family legacy" as a result. Sadly, this is sometimes Truth in Television , and can lead to years of bitterness from the less-successful sibling (older or younger). That said, because such feelings of resentment between siblings are often deeply personal and based on a multitude of factors, No Real Life Examples, Please! If the parents have been in a similarly successful position, this is called Generation Xerox , which will only add to the character's insecurity. This situation often causes Sibling Rivalry , and in the worst case to a Cain and Abel situation (i.e. the less successful sibling murdering the successful one). Compare Stronger Sibling , Dark Horse Sibling (when the successful sibling isn't the one you'd expect), "Well Done, Son" Guy (which occurs when a famous character seeks the approval of a relative, rather than a less successful person seeking attention from the general public) and Rich Sibling, Poor Sibling , which is about reality and/or material goods, rather than feeling inferior (although they can overlap). For more generalized examples of this, see: Lesser Star , Stuck in Their Shadow , Overshadowed by Awesome . Compare.
